title: Project Task
content:
Certinia PSA

Feature: Start Planned Project Task in Salesforce PSA

Page URL: https://ukgsf--stest.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/home

Login Flow

  • Navigate to the page URL.

  • Click "Salesforce Login" (if required).

  • Authenticate using SSO credentials.

  • After logging in, the Salesforce Home page is displayed.

 

Navigation Steps: 

Search and Open a Planned Project Task

  1. From the Salesforce Home page, the user clicks the Search bar at the top of the page.

  2. The user enters the project name “Perry's Restaurants, - Q-453446 - Ready Fixed Fee Implementation” in the search field and waits for result.

  3. User can select the searched project and the selected Project record opens successfully.

  4. The user clicks Show All in the related lists section.

  5. The user clicks on Project Tasks link

  6. In project task page, user clicks on Project Task where Status = Planned which will open project task detail page

  7. Wait for project task detailed page

 

AC1. Project Task Details Page

  • The Project Task record page is displayed after selecting a task from the Project's Project Tasks related list.

  • The page displays the following fields:

    • Status

    • Started checkbox

    • Actual Start Date

  • A Start button is visible when the task status is Planned.

Flow 2 — Start Project Task

  1. When user is in project task detailed page, and User clicks the Start button.

  2. The task is updated successfully.

  3. The page refreshes and displays:

    • Status = Started

    • Started = Checked

    • Actual Start Date = Current Date

 

AC2. Start Planned Project Task

  • The Start button is enabled for tasks with Status = Planned.

  • Clicking Start updates the task immediately.

  • The following field values are updated:

    • Status changes from Planned to Started.

    • Started checkbox is automatically selected.

    • Actual Start Date is populated with the current system date.

  • The changes are saved successfully and reflected on the Project Task page.
